cybershield_release: V52 Model Registry Foundation and Human-First Executive Control View
purpose: Defines the scoring models CyberShield should load and expose instead of hardcoding scoring logic inside index.html.

models.purpose: Evaluates whether an operational, AI, vendor, access, data, or governance action is controlled enough to proceed.
Evaluates whether a person, product, vendor, website, ad, source, brand claim, payment request, listing, communication, or AI-generated artifact is authentic enough to trust before someone acts.
Evaluates how defensible a decision record is based on evidence completeness, confidence, freshness, owner assignment, model versioning, and limitations.
Evaluates reliability, freshness, completeness, source quality, and conflict status of evidence.
Estimates directional exposure reduction when CyberShield identifies, constrains, escalates, or blocks a risky action before consequence.

builder_rule: Scores visible in the UI must route to model explanations. Do not create scores that cannot be explained.
limitations: V52 registry remains scaffold-level and demo-directional.
Complete model files should be created in a later local or connector-friendly build.
